questions.testName: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Scientists collected fallen acorns that each housed a colony of the ant species P. ohioensis and analyzed each colony's structure. For any of these colonies, if the colony has $x$ worker ants, the equation $y = 0.67 x + 2.6$, where $20 \leq x \leq 110$, gives the predicted number of larvae, $y$, in the colony. If one of these colonies has 41 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?
questions.questionText: Scientists collected fallen acorns that each housed a colony of the ant species P. ohioensis and analyzed each colony's structure. For any of these colonies, if the colony has $x$ worker ants, the equation $y = 0.67 x + 2.6$, where $20 \leq x \leq 110$, gives the predicted number of larvae, $y$, in the colony. If one of these colonies has 41 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?

questions.testName: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The graph models the number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six-month period, where $x$ is the number of six-month periods since the end of January 1995 and $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the point $(1, 500)$ in this context?
questions.questionText: The graph models the number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six-month period, where $x$ is the number of six-month periods since the end of January 1995 and $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the point $(1, 500)$ in this context?

choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers increased every six months by 500 subscribers.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1995 was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January was 500.

questions.testName: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In 2018, approximately 20,000 kilowatts of solar power capacity was newly installed in a certain area in the United States. In 2019, the amount of newly installed solar power capacity in this area increased by 13% from the amount of newly installed solar power capacity in 2018. If the amount of newly installed solar power capacity continues to increase each year by 13% of the previous year's newly installed solar power capacity, which of the following types of functions best describes how the amount of newly installed solar power capacity changes over time?
questions.questionText: In 2018, approximately 20,000 kilowatts of solar power capacity was newly installed in a certain area in the United States. In 2019, the amount of newly installed solar power capacity in this area increased by 13% from the amount of newly installed solar power capacity in 2018. If the amount of newly installed solar power capacity continues to increase each year by 13% of the previous year's newly installed solar power capacity, which of the following types of functions best describes how the amount of newly installed solar power capacity changes over time?

questions.testName: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: An object is launched into the air from ground level. According to a quadratic model, $1.3$ seconds after the object is launched, it reaches its maximum height of $27.04$ feet above ground level. Which equation represents this model, where $f(t)$ is the object's height, in feet, above ground level $t$ seconds after it was launched?
questions.questionText: An object is launched into the air from ground level. According to a quadratic model, $1.3$ seconds after the object is launched, it reaches its maximum height of $27.04$ feet above ground level. Which equation represents this model, where $f(t)$ is the object's height, in feet, above ground level $t$ seconds after it was launched?

questions.testName: May 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$f(x) = 7,000(1.005)^{2x}$
The given function $f$ models the balance of a bank account, in dollars, $x$ years after it is opened. Which statement is the best interpretation of $(1.005)^{2x}$?
questions.questionText:
$f(x) = 7,000(1.005)^{2x}$
The given function $f$ models the balance of a bank account, in dollars, $x$ years after it is opened. Which statement is the best interpretation of $(1.005)^{2x}$?

choices.text: Every 6 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
Every 2 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval.
At the end of every 2-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 2-month interval.

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A market researcher collected data on the price of a hot dog at 59 locations selected at random in a state. The mean price of a hot dog in the state was estimated to be \$3.28, with an associated margin of error of \$0.06. Which of the following is a plausible statement about the mean price of a hot dog for all locations that sell this product in the state?
questions.questionText: A market researcher collected data on the price of a hot dog at 59 locations selected at random in a state. The mean price of a hot dog in the state was estimated to be \$3.28, with an associated margin of error of \$0.06. Which of the following is a plausible statement about the mean price of a hot dog for all locations that sell this product in the state?

choices.text: It is either less than \$3.22 or greater than \$3.34.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error

questions.testName: June 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 7 kilograms traveling at a speed of $v$ meters per second is given by the function $K$, where $K(v)= \frac{7}{2} v^2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $K(24)= 2,016$ in this context?
questions.questionText: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 7 kilograms traveling at a speed of $v$ meters per second is given by the function $K$, where $K(v)= \frac{7}{2} v^2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $K(24)= 2,016$ in this context?

choices.text: The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.
The object traveling at 240 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.
The object traveling at 2,016 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.
The object traveling at 7,056 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.

choices.text: Every 6 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
Every 2 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval.
At the end of every 2-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 2-month interval.

questions.questionText: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 7 kilograms traveling at a speed of $v$ meters per second is given by the function $K$, where $K(v)= \frac{7}{2} v^2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $K(24)= 2,016$ in this context?

choices.text: The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.
The object traveling at 240 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.
The object traveling at 2,016 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.
The object traveling at 7,056 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules.
